turns.user_message: I completely blanked on a simple question during a quick conversation yesterday and just stood there staring at the floor. It’s strange how something so minor completely derailed my whole afternoon. I used to just laugh it off and move on without a second thought. Now I keep replaying the exact tone of my voice and wondering if I sounded completely foolish. I know it’s just a fleeting awkward moment, but the heavy feeling in my chest won’t really fade.

That same heavy feeling keeps creeping back in whenever I make even the slightest misstep in conversation. What used to be a quick brush-off now turns into hours of dissecting every word I said. I catch myself overthinking that brief pause from yesterday, convinced I left a terrible impression on everyone. I try to remind myself that people forget these things quickly, but my brain just won’t let the memory go. I’m so tired of carrying this invisible weight around.

That lingering awkwardness has completely taken over my headspace now, turning every minor slip into proof that I’m fundamentally flawed. I used to think I could just outgrow this habit of beating myself up over simple mistakes, but it just keeps digging deeper roots. I find myself freezing up before I even speak, terrified of messing up and confirming what I already suspect. It’s exhausting to constantly police my own thoughts and second-guess every single move I make. I feel permanently stained by my own clumsiness, and no amount of telling myself to move on actually fixes it.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This used to not bother the speaker.
